title: Python is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Python is a high-level, general-purpose programming language.

title: Java is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Java is a high-level, general-purpose, memory-safe, object-oriented programming language.

title: Scratch is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Scratch is a high-level, block-based visual programming language and website aimed primarily at children as an educational tool, with a target audience of ages 5 to 16.

title: R is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: R is a programming language for statistical computing and data visualization.

title: Go is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Go is a high-level, general-purpose programming language that is statically typed and compiled.

title: Assembly language is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: In computing, assembly language, often referred to simply as assembly and commonly abbreviated as ASM or asm, is any low-level programming language with a very strong correspondence between the instructions in the language and the architecture's machine code instructions.

title: PHP is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: PHP is a general-purpose scripting language geared towards web development.

title: TypeScript is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: TypeScript (TS) is a high-level programming language that adds static typing with optional type annotations to JavaScript.

title: XML is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Extensible Markup Language (XML) is a markup language and file format for storing, transmitting, and reconstructing data.

title: Cascading Style Sheets is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Cascading Style Sheets (CSS) is a style sheet language used for specifying the presentation and styling of a document written in a markup language, such as HTML or XML.

title: Fortran is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Fortran is a third-generation, compiled, imperative programming language designed for numeric computation and scientific computing.

title: Brainfuck is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Brainfuck is an esoteric programming language created in 1993 by Swiss student Urban Müller.

title: MATLAB is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: MATLAB is a proprietary multi-paradigm programming language and numeric computing environment developed by MathWorks.

title: Elixir is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Elixir is a functional, concurrent, high-level general-purpose programming language that runs on the BEAM virtual machine, which is also used to implement the Erlang programming language.

title: Lua is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Lua is a lightweight, high-level, multi-paradigm programming language designed mainly for embedded use in applications.

title: Zig is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Zig is a system programming language designed to be a general-purpose improvement to the C programming language.

title: Julia is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Julia is a dynamic general-purpose programming language.

title: WebAssembly is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: WebAssembly (Wasm) defines a portable binary code format and a corresponding text format for executable programs and software interfaces for facilitating communication between such programs and their host environment.

title: TeX is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: TeX, stylized as TeX, is a typesetting program that was designed and written by computer scientist and Stanford University professor Donald Knuth and first released in 1978.

title: F# is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: F# is a general-purpose, high-level, strongly typed, multi-paradigm programming language that encompasses functional, imperative, and object-oriented programming methods.

title: Smalltalk is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Smalltalk is a purely object-oriented programming language that was originally created in the 1970s for educational use, specifically for constructionist learning, but later found use in business.

title: Scala is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Scala is a strongly statically typed high-level general-purpose programming language that supports both object-oriented programming and functional programming.

title: AJAX is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: The Asynchronous JavaScript and XML, usually referred as Ajax is a set of web development techniques that uses various web technologies on the client-side to create asynchronous web applications.

title: Visual Basic is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Visual Basic (VB), originally called Visual Basic.NET (VB.NET), is a multi-paradigm, object-oriented programming language developed by Microsoft and implemented on.NET, Mono, and the.NET Framework.

title: APL is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: APL is a programming language developed in the 1960s by Kenneth E.

title: WebGL is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: WebGL is a JavaScript API for rendering interactive 2D and 3D graphics within any compatible web browser without the use of plug-ins.

title: Logo is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Logo is an educational programming language, designed in 1967 by Wally Feurzeig, Seymour Papert, and Cynthia Solomon.

title: OpenCL is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: OpenCL is a framework for writing programs that execute across heterogeneous platforms consisting of central processing units (CPUs), graphics processing units (GPUs), digital signal processors (DSPs), field-programmable gate arrays (FPGAs) and other processors or hardware accelerators.

title: Visual Basic is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Visual Basic (VB), sometimes referred to as Classic Visual Basic, is a third-generation programming language based on BASIC, as well as an associated integrated development environment (IDE).

title: Clojure is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Clojure is a dynamic and functional dialect of the programming language Lisp on the Java platform.

title: D is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: D, also known as dlang, is a multi-paradigm system programming language created by Walter Bright at Digital Mars and released in 2001.

title: Tcl is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Tcl is a high-level, general-purpose, interpreted, dynamic programming language.

title: Common Lisp is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Common Lisp (CL) is a dialect of the Lisp programming language, published in American National Standards Institute (ANSI) standard document ANSI INCITS 226-1994 (S2018).

title: Visual FoxPro is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Visual FoxPro is a programming language that was developed by Microsoft.

title: ALGOL is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: ALGOL is a family of imperative computer programming languages originally developed in 1958.

title: SPARQL is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: SPARQL is an RDF query language—that is, a semantic query language for databases—able to retrieve and manipulate data stored in Resource Description Framework (RDF) format.

title: VisiCalc is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: VisiCalc is the first spreadsheet computer program for personal computers, originally released for the Apple II by VisiCorp on October 17, 1979.

title: Bourne shell is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: The Bourne shell (sh) is a shell command-line interpreter for computer operating systems.

title: Mathematica is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Wolfram Mathematica is a software system with built-in libraries for several areas of technical computing that allows machine learning, statistics, symbolic computation, data manipulation, network analysis, time series analysis, NLP, optimization, plotting functions and various types of data, implementation of algorithms, creation of user interfaces, and interfacing with programs written in other programming languages.

title: Apache Groovy is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Apache Groovy is a Java-syntax-compatible object-oriented programming language for the Java platform.

title: GNU Octave is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: GNU Octave is a scientific programming language for scientific computing and numerical computation.

title: ActionScript is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: ActionScript is an object-oriented programming language originally developed by Macromedia Inc..

title: QBasic is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: QBasic is an integrated development environment (IDE) and interpreter for a variety of dialects of BASIC which are based on the QuickBASIC compiler and the QuickBASIC Interpreter.

title: Simula is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Simula is the name of two simulation programming languages, Simula I and Simula 67, developed in the 1960s at the Norwegian Computing Center in Oslo, by Ole-Johan Dahl and Kristen Nygaard.

title: Vala is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Vala is an object-oriented programming language with a self-hosting compiler that generates an intermediate representation in C source code and uses the GObject system.

title: Maple is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Maple is a symbolic and numeric computing environment as well as a multi-paradigm programming language.

title: INTERCAL is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: The Compiler Language With No Pronounceable Acronym (INTERCAL) is an esoteric programming language that was created as a parody by Don Woods and James M.

title: Mathcad is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Mathcad is computer software for the verification, validation, documentation and re-use of mathematical calculations in engineering and science, notably mechanical, chemical, electrical, and civil engineering.

title: REXX is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Rexx is a high-level programming language developed at IBM by Mike Cowlishaw.

title: AutoIt is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: AutoIt is a freeware programming language for Microsoft Windows.

title: Sass is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Sass is a preprocessor scripting language that is interpreted or compiled into Cascading Style Sheets (CSS).

title: Apache Ant is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Apache Ant is a software tool for automating software build processes for Java applications which originated from the Apache Tomcat project in early 2000 as a replacement for the Make build tool of Unix.

title: Gambas is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: Gambas is an object-oriented dialect of the BASIC programming language, and an integrated development environment that accompanies it.

title: FreeBASIC is created
subtitle: Programming or computer language
fact: FreeBASIC is a free and open source multiplatform compiler and programming language based on BASIC licensed under the GNU GPL for Microsoft Windows, protected-mode MS-DOS, Linux, FreeBSD and Xbox.
